concepts.name: NPPES entity-resolution benchmark: Dedupe.io vs Golden Suite
concepts.summary: Design a head-to-head entity-resolution benchmark using a 500k-row NPPES (CMS provider data) sample, comparing the canonical OSS tool Dedupe.io against GoldenMatch. Published as a blog post titled 'The OSS ER Bargain'.
reasoning: Chose NPPES (CMS Medicare/Medicaid provider data) as the dataset because it is public, large (6M+ rows), and has classic dirty-ER properties: name variants, address drift, org-vs-individual collisions.
Chose Dedupe.io as the sole OSS comparison tool (rejecting a multi-tool comparison including Splink and RecordLinkage) to keep the narrative clean — one canonical OSS tool vs Golden.
Sample size of 500k rows chosen over the full 6M to keep the run tractable; existing Golden Suite advancements are claimed to prevent OOM at this scale.

concepts.name: CCMS cluster comparison + sensitivity analysis for ground-truth-free ER evaluation
concepts.summary: Without a ground-truth linkage for NPPES, evaluate Dedupe vs GoldenMatch via (a) structural cluster agreement metrics like Adjusted Rand Index and pairwise-overlap and (b) per-tool sensitivity analysis that varies thresholds and observes cluster-shape stability.
reasoning: Running both tools on NPPES without ground truth means any raw precision/recall number would be fiction.
CCMS cluster comparison quantifies structural agreement (Adjusted Rand Index, pairwise overlap, cluster-size distributions) between the two tools' outputs — a defensible story even without labels.
Sensitivity analysis (varying thresholds / config knobs) shows which tool's clusters are stable under perturbation — orthogonal signal to the agreement metric.
Preferred over an alternate proposal (agreement-based comparison with manual spot-checks) because CCMS yields two independent quantitative axes instead of one.

concepts.name: Iterative spec-reviewer dispatch loop
concepts.summary: Write a design spec, dispatch a reviewer subagent against it, apply all blocking issues the reviewer finds, then re-dispatch on the revised spec and iterate until the reviewer returns no blockers. Same pattern repeats for the implementation plan.
reasoning: First-pass spec draft flagged 12 issues by the reviewer, including scope and feasibility blockers.
Rewriting the spec to address every issue and re-running the reviewer produced a clean second-pass approval — the iterate-until-clean loop is the design pattern.
Implementation plan (separate artifact) goes through the same loop; plan-reviewer found 2 blocking issues (retrain-per-subprocess, invented GM API) which were patched in a second pass.
